response.justification: While the candidate has valuable geospatial domain expertise and proven system design maturity, the primary job requirement—modern C++ development—is critically unmet. Their last professional C++ work ended in 2008 (18 years ago), and their recent career focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Without current C++ or Rust skills, and lacking clear navigation routing pipeline experience (as opposed to general geo-mapping), the onboarding risk for this specialized role is too high despite strong domain alignment.
strengths: Direct geospatial/mapping domain expertise from founding and maintaining GISCollective, a production geo-data platform used by conservation organizations
Proven high-performance distributed systems experience: Dunnhumby role handling millions of HTTP requests per second with real-time data processing
Staff-level engineering maturity demonstrated across multiple senior roles with strong system design thinking and architecture experience
concerns: C++ expertise severely outdated: last professional C++ work ended in 2008; job requires modern C++ as primary skill; no demonstrated recent proficiency
No Rust experience available as required alternative; current primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not systems languages
Navigation routing pipeline experience unclear: resume shows geo-data mapping/collection work (GISCollective, Green Map) rather than production routing optimization or traffic integration systems
